methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Agility Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D)

private.name: Gecko Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Exotec is a Series D robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Romain Moulin and Renaud Heitz, currently valued at $2.0B post-money following $335M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including Divergent, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and Hadrian spanning adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Exotec's $2.0B Series D valuation and applying a typical 1.5x multiplier for progression to the next funding stage, implies $3.0B. Peer-median valuation across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C)—yields a median of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $2.3B. These methods do not converge tightly, reflecting stage dispersion within the peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $3.4B (high), with a median of $2.3B. The current $2.0B valuation sits within this range but toward the lower-median end. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.5x stage multiplier for this sector and the limited peer set at exactly Series D stage, both of which warrant sensitivity analysis in any final valuation opinion.
